key_insight: SimPhysNet combines physics-informed self-supervised learning with few-shot prototypical classification to predict laser weld penetration accurately from very few labeled images.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical, low-label deployment of penetration-state monitoring for more reliable, automated laser welding quality control.
method: Embed physical priors via a PINN to learn meaningful molten-pool and keyhole features from unlabeled images
Use contrastive self-supervised learning with three image augmentation tasks to improve representation quality
Apply prototypical-network few-shot learning to classify penetration state from a small labeled set
evidence_signal: The model reached 96.06% classification accuracy using only 200 labeled images, about 5% of the labeled dataset, and was reported to be comparable to conventional fully supervised methods.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the learned physics priors and augmentations transfer across different welding setups, materials, and imaging conditions.
paper_title: A welding penetration prediction model for laser welding process based on self-supervised learning using physics-informed neural networks
